intro.title: Dumbo Health 30-Day Sleep Improvement Plan
intro.description: Build better sleep in 30 days. Week 1: Foundation. Week 2: Sleep–bed connection. Week 3: Calm your mind. Week 4: Lock in success.

day-1.title: Day 1: Set Your Wake-Up Time
day-1.description: Choose a wake-up time you can stick to every single day even weekends and set your alarm for that time starting tomorrow morning.

day-2.title: Day 2: Start Your Sleep Diary
day-2.description: Tonight, start tracking your sleep. Note bed time, when you fell asleep, any wake-ups, and when you woke up.

day-3.title: Day 3: Assess Your Sleep Environment
day-3.description: Walk into your bedroom tonight and notice everything. Is it dark enough? Too warm? Make one change to improve your sleep space.

day-4.title: Day 4: Cut the Caffeine Cutoff
day-4.description: No caffeine after 2 PM today. That includes coffee, tea, energy drinks, and even chocolate.

day-5.title: Day 5: Lower the Pressure Around Sleep
day-5.description: Tonight, practice letting go of the need to force sleep. Your only job right now is to rest your body.

day-6.title: Day 6: Create a Wind-Down Ritual
day-6.description: Starting one hour before bed, do the same 3 calming activities in the same order. Dim lights, herbal tea, stretch, read, or soft music.

day-7.title: Day 7: Reflect on Week 1
day-7.description: Review your sleep diary from the past week. Notice trends: Are you falling asleep faster? How do you feel during the day?

day-8.title: Day 8: The 15-Minute Rule
day-8.description: If you can't fall asleep within 15–20 minutes, get out of bed. Do something calm and boring, then return when you feel sleepy.

day-9.title: Day 9: Keep Your Bed Sacred
day-9.description: Use your bed only for sleep (and intimacy). No scrolling, no TV, no work emails.

day-10.title: Day 10: Master the Middle-of-the-Night Wake-Up
day-10.description: If you wake and can't fall back asleep within 15–20 minutes, get up. Do a calm activity, then return to bed when sleepy.

day-11.title: Day 11: Set a Bedtime Window
day-11.description: Only go to bed when you're truly sleepy not just tired or bored. Notice the difference between tired and sleepy.

day-12.title: Day 12: Limit Liquids Before Bed
day-12.description: Stop drinking fluids 90 minutes before bedtime to reduce nighttime trips to the bathroom.

day-13.title: Day 13: Practice the Sleepy Walk
day-13.description: If you get out of bed tonight, move slowly and calmly. Keep lights dim. Walk like you're still half-asleep.

day-14.title: Day 14: Week 2 Reflection
day-14.description: Review your sleep diary. Calculate sleep efficiency: (Total Sleep Time ÷ Time in Bed) × 100. Aim for 85% or higher.

day-15.title: Day 15: Try Progressive Muscle Relaxation
day-15.description: Before bed, tense each muscle group for 5 seconds then release, from toes to head. Use a guided audio if helpful.

day-16.title: Day 16: The Worry Dump
day-16.description: One hour before bed, write down everything on your mind worries, to-dos, random thoughts. Then close the notebook and leave it in another room.

day-17.title: Day 17: Challenge Catastrophic Thinking
day-17.description: Replace thoughts like I'll never sleep with: I've had bad nights before and managed. I'll get through tomorrow.

day-18.title: Day 18: Use the 4-7-8 Breathing Technique
day-18.description: Breathe in for 4 counts, hold for 7, exhale for 8. Repeat 4 times. Activates your body's relaxation response.

day-19.title: Day 19: Visualize Your Calm Place
day-19.description: As you lie in bed, imagine a place where you feel completely safe and relaxed. Picture every detail sights, sounds, smells.

day-20.title: Day 20: Give Yourself Permission to Rest
day-20.description: Remind yourself: Even if I don't fall asleep right away, rest is still valuable. My body is healing and recharging.

day-21.title: Day 21: Week 3 Reflection
day-21.description: Review your week. Which relaxation or cognitive technique felt most helpful? Write down your top 2 to use moving forward.

day-22.title: Day 22: Optimize Your Sleep Schedule
day-22.description: Adjust your bedtime so you're in bed only as long as you're actually sleeping. Match bed time to your average sleep duration.

day-23.title: Day 23: Plan for Off Nights
day-23.description: Accept that not every night will be perfect. Make a plan for what to do when sleep doesn't come easily.

day-24.title: Day 24: Move Your Body
day-24.description: Get at least 20–30 minutes of movement today walking, stretching, yoga. Finish at least 3 hours before bed.

day-25.title: Day 25: Mindful Eating Before Bed
day-25.description: Avoid heavy, spicy, or large meals within 3 hours of bedtime. If you need a snack, choose something light and sleep-friendly.

day-26.title: Day 26: Review Your Wins
day-26.description: Look back at your sleep diary from Day 1. Compare to this week. What's improved? Write down 3 improvements you've noticed.

day-27.title: Day 27: Create Your Maintenance Plan
day-27.description: List the 5 habits from this program that made the biggest difference. These are your non-negotiables moving forward.

day-28.title: Day 28: Handle Travel and Schedule Changes
day-28.description: Make a plan for how you'll protect your sleep during travel, late events, and stress.

day-29.title: Day 29: Express Gratitude for Your Sleep
day-29.description: Tonight before bed, think of 3 things you're grateful for about your sleep journey.

day-30.title: Day 30: Celebrate and Commit
day-30.description: Review your entire 30-day journey. Celebrate your progress, download your certificate, and commit to continuing the habits that work.

conclusion.title: Conclusion: Keep Your Sleep Habits Alive
conclusion.description: Sleep improvement is a lifestyle. Continue your non-negotiable habits and return to this plan whenever you need a reset.
